title: adina fagifolia
intro: Adina fagifolia, also known as the Myanmar tea tree or Burmese tea plant, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Adina fagifolia include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. The plant is traditionally prepared as a tea or infusions, which can also be used in the form of ointments or salves for topical application.
